metric_value: Primary focus: PSG and IPG; as Chief Research Officer, covers HP as a company.
HP corporate messages have been 'very simplistic' and not communicated or drilled down at AR/analyst-facing events.
HP message of 'low total-cost-of-ownership provider' — not heard, not believable.
Reduced-complexity message: believable on consumer side; lesser on IPG; no proof points on ESG; only marginal on services.
Adaptive Enterprise is too IT-facing and too little business-executive-management-facing.
Adaptive Enterprise is an ESG and Services initiative; PSG and IPG not tied in.
Analysts want beef — 'too much vision and not enough what-can-I-do-and-how-can-I-do-it.'
Neither HP Adaptive Enterprise nor IBM On Demand winning mindshare; On Demand 'too web-services-oriented and not enough infrastructure-metric-oriented.'
Adaptive Enterprise essentially says 'Rip and Replace.'
HP Services can handle individual projects but lacks capacity for Fortune 500 company transformation.
ESG new management due to lack of profitability; PSG barely profitable; IPG very profitable and executing well (high-end printing could do more); consumer space doing best but not profitably.
HP was very late to market with Centrino-based notebooks; has products but has not earned stripes in 'Mobility as a lifestyle.'
HP is a leader in digital imaging and photography.
HP is a technology components company moving to technology solutions; least believable label is 'business solutions company.'
HP has not demonstrated to analysts that customers achieve business value from HP relationships; not a subject in briefings.
Too much HP business comes from installed base — implies trouble generating new customers.
HP's capability to measure and maintain IT-to-business value-chain linkage is spotty — not a core competency.
HP heterogeneous integration is adequate but not world-leading; modest IBM-mainframe integration skills.
HP has Unix expertise and growing Linux share but is underplaying the Microsoft market.
'Products that work seamlessly together' across all geographies/product lines is not believable; IPG in SMB doing good delivery/satisfaction.
HP SMB partner network could be better; still 4 different stovepipes at divisional level; SAP solutions initiative earns B-.
HP tries to buy channel partner loyalty — probably not enough.
HP is not a full-spectrum camera company; no interchangeable-lens model; well-focused for consumers.
HP has not adequately differentiated Compaq and HP brands in retail channel; marketing dollars not effective; Dell ruthlessly focused.
Not yet the leader in connectivity/end-to-end processing ('radical simplicity'); Internet Home Alliance research showed more customer-faced interaction needed.
HP is dropping the ball by waiting for home networked video market to evolve — letting Sony and others set standards.
[UNVERIFIED]
HP consumer service and support only mediocre; echoed by big PC-magazine survey; 'HP's PCs break too often.'
HP 'wears so many different hats'; Carly has different messages for consumer, IT exec, and enterprise exec audiences — of necessity.
PSG and IPG are working a little bit better together ('better together' cited as HP theme).
HP enterprise 'Demand More' message is telling buyers to demand more of their IT.
HP SMB 'Get More' message: get more from relationship with HP, demand more, use more.
